acceptance_targets: [ch16-craft-001] PASS if, on a close read of the revised beats around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263, the mirror either shows only one memory or an explicit in-text rule exception is established before the second display. FAIL if Mrs. Kroll's mirror still presents two separate witnessed memories with no rule change.

[ch16-prose-001] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:307, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:335, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:345, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:489 no longer stack three or more emphasis beats in the same clipped-negation mold and at least three cited moments are recast as complete clauses or sentence forms distinct from one another. FAIL if the chapter still relies on this same fragment pattern at three or more of those cited beats.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, on a close read of the revised beats around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263, the mirror either shows only one memory or an explicit in-text rule exception is established before the second display. FAIL if Mrs. Kroll's mirror still presents two separate witnessed memories with no rule change.
findings.problem: The same dresser mirror produces a witnessed dust-memory when Mrs. Kroll buys it, then produces another distinct memory later in the public reckoning. That contradicts the established rule that a dust-memory on a given surface fades once clearly witnessed, turning the climax's magical proof into a canon break rather than an earned escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise either the first mirror manifestation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123 or the later cluster at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263 so the mirror only carries one witnessed memory. Best fix: keep the early mirror flare or the later public display, not both, and reassign the removed image beat to an untouched surface already present in the room if you still need multiple proofs.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:307, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:335, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:345, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:489 no longer stack three or more emphasis beats in the same clipped-negation mold and at least three cited moments are recast as complete clauses or sentence forms distinct from one another. FAIL if the chapter still relies on this same fragment pattern at three or more of those cited beats.
findings.problem: The narration leans repeatedly on clipped negative fragments for emphasis: "Not with prayer or apology. With terms."; "Not because she had reached the end."; "Not a confession... Not a lie either."; "Not relieved."; "Not stop loving, not stop grieving, not pardon." By the fourth and fifth use, the move stops feeling sharp and starts reading like autopilot. It also brushes against the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the fragment-heavy emphasis beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:307, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:335, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:345, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:489 into varied, grammatically complete sentence shapes. Keep the pressure, but let at least three of these moments land through image, cadence, or action instead of another clipped negation pattern.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into queer secrecy, erotic specificity, dust-sick bodies, economic shame, bad bargains, and the humiliations of dependence. When deciding between a tidier scene and a truer one, choose the version that preserves historical pressure and emotional contradiction even if it leaves characters compromised, unsympathetic, or exposed.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If grief, desire, debt, illness, bodily damage, or shame are abstracted at the moment they should become sensory and social, rewrite toward the concrete consequence until the scene stops hiding from itself.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: moderate-high - contractions are the baseline for speech; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, social performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural rhythms across the book: survival set-piece, aftermath inventory, intimate chamber scene, bureaucratic squeeze, outdoor search, quiet communal ritual, preparation without payoff, public reckoning, and departure elegy. Vary scene counts, dialogue density, and where the turn comes from so consecutive chapters never feel built from the same dramatic machine.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken and period-credible, with agricultural, domestic, and biblical undertones used sparingly; favor exact nouns and verbs over decorative frontier lyricism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person rotating among Cora, Thea, Dell, August, and Hester, with only brief collective widening when weather or communal memory demands it
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive prose: longer weather and labor sentences that can suddenly snap short at revelation, pain, or shame.
